Amit Gohel is an AI Engineer focused on shipping **production AI systems** (not just prototypes). In **under 2 years of professional experience**, he has built **6 end-to-end AI systems** that are live and serving real users, including:
- A **permission-aware RAG chatbot** handling **50K+ queries/month**

- A **video course builder** that reduced production time from **1 week to 10 minutes**

He has shipped across the full spectrum—from **hardware + ML** (e.g., **TinyML devices with ESP32 and Arduino**, soldering **IMU sensors onto finger rings**) to **multi-model LLM architectures** in production using **GPT-4o, Gemini, and Qwen3-Coder**. title: Achievements
content:
## Achievements, Hackathons & Competitions — Amit Gohel

### Samsung Solve for Tomorrow — Top 10 Finalist (70,000+ participants)
- **Project:** “Voicer” — a wearable AI device that translates **Indian Sign Language** into **spoken audio in real time**.
- **Hardware/ML stack:** **10 IMU sensors** worn as **finger rings** + **NodeMCU microcontroller** running a **custom-trained neural network**.
- **Model performance:** **94.1% accuracy** across **12 gesture classes**.
- **Output/Connectivity:** Speech output via a **mobile app over Bluetooth**.
- **Recognition:** **Top 10 finalist** nationwide among **70,000+ participants** in Samsung’s flagship innovation competition; **featured on CNN News18 national television** during **Season 2**.
- **Amit Gohel’s role:** Designed and trained the **TinyML** model; generated the **custom dataset (200 samples per class from 10 participants)**; assembled all hardware; optimized the model to fit within **55KB flash** and **2KB RAM**.

### Smart India Hackathon 2023 — Top 5 Finalist (44,000+ teams)
- **Event:** Hardware Edition at **MIT Karnataka**.
- **Project:** Automated **drug dispenser system** to reduce medication errors in healthcare settings.
- **System components:**
  - **QR code authentication:** **ESP32-CAM** scans a QR code to verify the patient and retrieve their prescription.
  - **Precision medication dispensing:** **Arduino** with **stepper motors**; each compartment dispenses the exact prescribed quantity.
  - **Real-time multi-compartment synchronization:** multiple drug compartments coordinate to prepare a complete medication dose.
- **Problem addressed:** Medication dispensing errors in hospitals/pharmacies, especially high-volume settings where manual dispensing is error-prone and time-consuming.
- **Amit Gohel’s role:** Hardware integration and embedded systems programming—wired **ESP32-CAM** and **Arduino** with stepper motors; implemented **QR code scanning and authentication logic**; synchronized **multi-compartment dispensing**.

### Ingenium Hackathon — 2nd Place (200+ teams)
- **Project:** “Saransh” — a **Raspberry Pi-based** device converting physical book pages into **multilingual audio summaries**.
- **End-to-end pipeline:**
  1. **Image capture:** Camera module captures a photo of the book page.
  2. **Text extraction:** **Tesseract OCR** extracts text from the image.
  3. **Summary generation:** **NLP pipeline using Transformers** generates a concise summary.
  4. **Multilingual audio output:** **Text-to-speech** converts the summary into audio in multiple languages.
- **Use case:** Accessibility tool for **visually impaired users**, **language learners**, or anyone needing quick audio summaries of printed material without manual typing/scanning.
- **Amit Gohel’s role:** Built the end-to-end **Python** pipeline—OCR integration, NLP summarization, and audio output; integrated all software components with Raspberry Pi hardware.

### Pattern Across Competitions (Amit Gohel)
Across all three projects, Amit Gohel consistently deployed **AI/ML models on physical hardware** to solve real-world problems for underserved communities—**sign language users**, **hospital patients**, and **visually impaired individuals**—emphasizing hardware-integrated AI rather than purely software-based AI projects.

title: What Sets Amit Apart
content:
## Profile: What Sets Amit Apart — Why Hire Amit Over Other Candidates

Amit (AI/ML engineer, ~2 years experience) emphasizes unusual full-stack range: trained ML models in notebooks, built web apps calling OpenAI APIs, **assembled hardware**, **deployed models on microcontrollers with 2KB RAM**, built **production RAG systems handling 50K queries/month**, designed **multi-model LLM architectures**, and **shipped 6 end-to-end AI systems** used daily by real users. Scope spans from soldering sensors to orchestrating **GPT-4o + Gemini + Qwen3-Coder** in production.

### 1) Ships production systems, not demos
Amit highlights production realities handled directly: latency, permission systems, concurrent users, cost optimization, error handling, edge cases. Examples of systems in production:

| System | Production Scale |
|---|---|
| **RAG Chatbot (Silver Touch)** | **50K+ queries/month**, thousands of clients, **10+ chatbot instances** |
| **RAG Chatbot (Vivansh)** | **500+ concurrent users**, ~**1,000 queries/day**, **250+ permissions enforced per query** |
| **Form Builder** | **200+ forms generated in first month**, used daily by client team and tenants |
| **Course Builder** | Replaced **1-week manual process**, active in production |

Production issues Amit has worked through include: debugging why **MRR dropped after a data update**, diagnosing **AWS Lambda timeouts** on certain video formats, and resolving edge cases where user permission profiles create ambiguous access scenarios.

### 2) Builds across the entire AI stack (shipped at each layer)

| Layer | What Amit has built |
|---|---|
| **Hardware** | Custom sensor rigs (IMU rings, wristbands), 3D-printed enclosures, C++ firmware, I2C wiring |
| **Edge/Embedded ML** | Models on Arduino Nano (**1MB flash**), NodeMCU, ESP32 — quantized to **INT8**, running in **2ms** |
| **Data Engineering** | Custom datasets: **12,400 gesture samples**, **2+ hours labeled audio**, **10,000+ video clips indexed** |
| **ML Training** | **1,000+** model architecture search; CNN-BiLSTM hybrids; 1D CNN + GRU; hyperparameter sweeps |
| **Retrieval Systems** | Production RAG with **Milvus**, multi-index architectures, reranking, permission-aware filtering |
| **LLM Applications** | Multi-model orchestration (**GPT-4o + Gemini + Qwen3-Coder**), structured output, intermediate schemas |
| **Infrastructure** | **AWS Lambda** serverless pipelines, on-premise LLM hosting, **vLLM** model serving |
| **Document Processing** | Hybrid OCR pipelines, PDF parsing, entity extraction, markdown/JSON conversion |

### 3) Data-driven technical decisions (benchmarked, not trend-driven)
- **Vector database benchmarks (production data):** FAISS (**45% MRR**) vs Qdrant (**85%**) vs Milvus (**95%**) → selected **Milvus**
- **Embedding models tested:** BGE small/base/large, Stella small/large, SPLADE, **BGE-M3** → selected **BGE-M3** for retrieval quality
- **Blueprint generation:** GPT-4o couldn’t produce structurally accurate form blueprints → tested multiple models; **Qwen3-Coder-480B** won empirically
- **OCR:** no single engine sufficient → combined **DocTR + DocLink + LLM merging** after individual testing
- **Air writing model architecture:** **1,000+ configurations** (Dense, CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, hybrids) → proved best through exhaustive search
- **Infant cry audio features:** compared **MFE vs MFCC vs Spectrogram** → **MFE** won on accuracy

### 4) Designs for real-world complexity
- **Permission-aware RAG:** same question yields different answers per user with **250+ permissions**; **no caching possible**. Handles compound queries (e.g., “add and edit user”) by splitting—permitted parts answered and restricted parts denied inline. Includes coreference resolution across turns (e.g., “How do I edit one?” → “How do I edit a user?”) with permission re-validation every turn.
- **Multi-model orchestration (Form Builder):** uses **3 LLMs** in one user flow because each is best at a specific subtask. Includes handoff design, intermediate output validation with **Pydantic**, and failure handling at each stage.
- **Hardware constraints:** fit a **12-class neural network** into **55KB flash** and **2KB RAM** on a microcontroller; run in **2ms**. Architecture chosen for accuracy *and* device fit.
- **On-premise requirements:** full OCR + LLM pipeline running on a client **250GB server**, **zero external API calls** (no OpenAI, no cloud OCR; data never leaves the building). Every component self-hosted.

### 5) External validation and achievements

- **CNN News18:** national television feature for **Voicer**
